section: Chapter 8: Force and Laws of Motion

questions.question: What is force?
questions.correct_answer: A push or pull acting on an object
questions.explanation: Force is an interaction that can change the state of motion or shape of an object.

questions.question: What is meant by balanced forces?
questions.correct_answer: Forces that are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction
questions.explanation: Balanced forces cancel each other and do not change the state of motion of an object.

questions.question: What are unbalanced forces?
questions.correct_answer: Forces that cause a change in the state of motion of an object
questions.explanation: When forces acting on an object are unequal, they produce motion or acceleration.

questions.question: What is inertia?
questions.correct_answer: The tendency of an object to resist change in its state of motion or rest
questions.explanation: Inertia depends on the mass of the object. Greater mass means greater inertia.

questions.question: What is Newton’s First Law of Motion?
questions.correct_answer: An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an unbalanced force
questions.explanation: This law explains the concept of inertia.

questions.question: What is momentum?
questions.correct_answer: The product of mass and velocity of an object
questions.explanation: Momentum is calculated using the formula p = mv.

questions.question: What is Newton’s Second Law of Motion?
questions.correct_answer: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the applied force
questions.explanation: This law gives the mathematical relation F = ma.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.explanation: One Newton is the force required to produce an acceleration of 1 m/s² in a mass of 1 kg.

questions.question: What is Newton’s Third Law of Motion?
questions.correct_answer: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction
questions.explanation: Action and reaction forces act on different objects simultaneously.

questions.question: Why do passengers fall forward when a moving bus stops suddenly?
questions.explanation: The body continues to move forward even when the bus stops suddenly.

notes.title: Force Definition
notes.content: Force is a push or pull that can change the speed, direction, or shape of an object.

notes.title: Balanced and Unbalanced Forces
notes.content: Balanced forces do not change motion, while unbalanced forces cause acceleration or change in direction.

notes.title: Inertia
notes.content: Inertia is the resistance of an object to change its state of rest or motion and depends on its mass.

notes.title: Momentum
notes.content: Momentum is the quantity of motion possessed by a moving object and is given by p = mv.

notes.title: Newton’s Laws of Motion
notes.content: The three laws of motion explain how forces affect the motion of objects.

facts.fact: The SI unit of force is Newton (N).
Momentum depends on both mass and velocity of an object.
Inertia increases with the mass of an object.
Action and reaction forces are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction.
